description: Add validation for parent_id before using it in Notion transaction operations to prevent corrupted transactions with empty block IDs.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/notion && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Validate parent_id in create-database-item and delete-page before transaction
userStories.description: As a user, I want Notion tools to fail gracefully with a clear error when a page or database has no parent_id instead of sending corrupted transactions with empty block IDs.
acceptanceCriteria: create-database-item.ts validates that parentId is non-empty after extracting it from collData, throwing ToolError.internal if empty
delete-page.ts validates that parentId is non-empty after extracting it from pageData, throwing ToolError.internal if empty
The error messages clearly describe what went wrong (parent_id is missing from the database/page record)
cd plugins/notion && npm run build passes
cd plugins/notion && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/notion && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/notion && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Two files need the same fix pattern:

1. plugins/notion/src/tools/create-database-item.ts line 41:
```
const parentId = (collData.parent_id as string) ?? '';
```
This defaults to '' if parent_id is missing. Then at line 92, parentId is used in a transaction:
```
pointer: { table: 'block', id: parentId, spaceId },
command: 'listAfter',
```
An empty id causes the transaction to target a nonexistent block, silently corrupting the database structure.

Fix: After line 41, add:
```
if (!parentId) throw ToolError.internal(`Database ${params.database_id} has no parent_id — cannot add item to parent content list`);
```

2. plugins/notion/src/tools/delete-page.ts line 32:
```
const parentId = (pageData.parent_id as string) ?? '';
```
Same issue — at line 50, parentId is used in a listRemove transaction.

Fix: After line 32, add:
```
if (!parentId) throw ToolError.internal(`Page ${params.page_id} has no parent_id — cannot remove from parent content list`);
```

ToolError is already imported in both files.
